title: Hosted Website And Cloud Control Plane

depends_on.reason: The hosted website should build on stable site/tent/zone/device identities and command idempotency rather than exposing singleton hardware APIs directly.

schema_basis.summary: This JSON is shaped for long-running, resumable agent work: it has explicit milestones, observable acceptance criteria, update rules, decision logs, and validation commands. It favors simple fixed workflows over unnecessary autonomous complexity.

sources.title: OpenAI Cookbook: Using PLANS.md for multi-hour problem solving
principles_used: Make the plan self-contained for future agent sessions.
Keep progress and decisions inside the plan.
Use observable acceptance criteria and exact commands.
Treat the plan as a living document.

sources.title: Anthropic Engineering: Building effective agents
principles_used: Use the simplest composable architecture that fits the task.
Prefer predictable workflows for well-defined work.

sources.title: AWS Durable Execution SDK: Idempotency and retries
principles_used: Assume retries and interruption are normal.

purpose.problem: The frontend is currently coupled to local services running on this box. Exposing it publicly requires hosted auth, hosted APIs, and a cloud-readable state path without making Vercel responsible for hardware control.
purpose.outcome: A Vercel-hosted frontend and thin API show Dirt state remotely and submit durable command intent while this box remains the only hardware authority.
purpose.non_goal: Do not move hardware loops, sensor ingestion, camera control, humidifier/fan safety logic, or local fallback automation to Vercel.

target_topology: Cloud Postgres or equivalent hosted database
Object storage for photos/video with signed URLs
Outbound-only local site gateway running on this box
architecture.control_plane_rule: The hosted API records intent, serves remote UI data, handles auth, and provides signed asset URLs. It does not execute hardware actions directly.
architecture.data_plane_rule: The local site gateway reads local state, pushes telemetry/assets/results to cloud, pulls queued commands, validates them locally, executes only safe commands, and reports final status.
architecture.connectivity_rule: The local box should initiate outbound connections. Avoid opening inbound router ports for normal operation.
architecture.realtime_rule: Start with simple HTTP polling and event-shaped records. Upgrade to a broker or realtime service only after measured latency or scale needs justify it.

off_limits: web-ui/src/api-client/generated/** except via the repository contract generation command
hardware service behavior that keeps plants safe unless explicitly in scope
repository_context.dirty_worktree_warning: Before editing, run git status --short and inspect overlapping user changes. Do not revert unrelated changes.

execution_rules.one_session_rule: A future agent should complete one milestone or one explicitly selected task per session unless the user asks for broader execution.
execution_rules.plan_update_rule: When work starts, set this plan status to in-progress and update the relevant milestone/task status. When stopping, update progress_log with changed files, validation, deployment notes, and next task.
execution_rules.decision_rule: Record provider choices, auth model choices, sync semantics, and command safety decisions in decision_log.
execution_rules.surprise_rule: Record platform limits, deployment blockers, network assumptions, and local service incompatibilities in surprises_and_discoveries.
execution_rules.completion_rule: Set status to done only when the hosted frontend and API are deployed or a user-approved local equivalent passes all acceptance criteria.

milestones.title: Platform Decisions
milestones.goal: Choose the smallest hosted stack that satisfies auth, data persistence, asset storage, deployment, and outbound local sync.

tasks.description: Confirm current Vercel limits for Functions, body sizes, background work, cron, environment variables, and supported runtimes from official Vercel docs.
Choose hosted Postgres/storage/auth providers or document why existing local services remain the temporary source of truth.
Decide whether the thin API is implemented as Vercel Functions, a separately hosted FastAPI service, or a hybrid.
Record deployment environments, secrets, and ownership boundaries in decision_log.

acceptance_criteria: decision_log records chosen hosting, database, object storage, auth/session, and sync approach.
The chosen design does not require inbound access to this box for normal operation.
Known Vercel/platform limits are documented in surprises_and_discoveries or decision_log with links.

validation.command: No code validation required for this milestone.
validation.expected_result: Plan updated with concrete provider decisions and rationale.

milestones.title: Cloud Data Contract
milestones.goal: Define the cloud-readable subset of Dirt state and the durable command/event records shared with the local site gateway.

tasks.description: Define cloud tables or schemas for sites, tents, zones, devices, telemetry summaries, latest state, command inbox, command results, asset metadata, alerts, and audit events.
Define command statuses and idempotency semantics compatible with the multi-tent-controller plan.
Define stale-state fields such as last_seen_at, source_updated_at, synced_at, and offline indicators.
Decide retention and aggregation policy so raw high-volume telemetry does not make the hosted UI slow or expensive.

acceptance_criteria: Cloud schema can represent current main tent and future breeding tent under one site.
Command records are durable, target-scoped, idempotent, expirable, and auditable.
The UI can distinguish live, stale, offline, failed, rejected, and expired command/state conditions.
Photos/video are represented as metadata plus object storage keys, not raw blobs through Vercel Functions.

validation.expected_result: Schema/service tests pass if repository code is changed.

milestones.title: Local Site Gateway Sync
milestones.goal: Add an outbound-only local sync process that publishes telemetry/assets/results and consumes cloud command intent.

tasks.description: Implement a local sync service or module that reads local Dirt state and sends site/tent scoped updates to the cloud API.
Add a local durable outbox so telemetry, asset metadata, and command results survive internet loss and process restart.
Poll or otherwise fetch queued cloud commands, validate them locally, execute through existing local services, and publish final command status.
Add systemd unit or documented run command for the sync process if it is a new service.

acceptance_criteria: The local gateway can run with cloud offline, queue outbound events, and flush them later without duplication.
The local gateway refuses expired, duplicate, out-of-range, or unsafe commands and records the reason.
The gateway authenticates to the cloud using a scoped device/site credential, not a browser user token.
Existing local hardware safety loops continue to run if the sync service is stopped.

validation.command: uv run pytest apps/shared/tests apps/hwd/tests apps/web/tests -q
validation.expected_result: Gateway, outbox, and command tests pass.

milestones.title: Hosted API
milestones.goal: Expose a thin hosted API for browser auth, dashboard reads, command creation, sync ingestion, and signed asset URL creation.

tasks.description: Implement hosted API routes for auth/session, current state reads, telemetry history reads, command creation/status reads, edge sync ingestion, and signed asset URLs.
Ensure browser-facing routes enforce user authorization and edge-facing routes enforce scoped device authentication.
Ensure command creation only records intent; hardware execution remains local.
Add tests and mock fixtures for offline/stale state, rejected commands, and duplicate command submissions.

acceptance_criteria: Authenticated browser users can read current state and command status from the hosted API.
The hosted API cannot directly reach local hardware services.
Duplicate command creation with the same idempotency key returns the existing command.
Edge ingestion endpoints reject missing, invalid, or over-scoped credentials.
Large media uploads/downloads use signed object storage flows.

validation.expected_result: Hosted API tests pass if implemented in the Python service.
Frontend/API client tests pass if implemented in the web workspace.

milestones.title: Vercel Frontend
milestones.goal: Deploy the React/Vite frontend to Vercel with remote-safe state, auth, and environment configuration.

tasks.description: Configure web-ui for hosted API base URLs, production environment variables, and preview deployments.
Update UI data fetching to use hosted API/client contracts rather than assuming same-origin local dirt-web.
Add UI states for offline site gateway, stale sensor data, queued/running/succeeded/failed/rejected/expired commands, and disabled unsafe actions.
Use MSW v2 or test fixtures to exercise hosted/offline/stale scenarios in frontend tests.

acceptance_criteria: Production build passes and can be deployed to Vercel.
The hosted UI displays current main tent and future breeding tent identity from the API.
The UI visibly distinguishes stale/offline data from live data.
The UI never embeds hardware credentials, device tokens, or local-only secrets.
Actuator controls create command intent and then render command lifecycle status.

milestones.title: Deployment And Operations
milestones.goal: Make the hosted system deployable, observable, and recoverable without putting plants at risk.

tasks.description: Document required environment variables, secret rotation steps, local gateway credentials, and Vercel project configuration.
Add operational checks for gateway last_seen_at, sync backlog depth, command failures, and API health.
Verify local fallback behavior by stopping network/cloud sync while confirming hardware automation remains local.
Update outcomes_and_retrospective with final deployment URLs, remaining risks, and rollback steps.

acceptance_criteria: A remote user can log in, view current state, view history/assets, submit an allowed command, and see the final local execution status.
If the local gateway is offline, the UI shows stale/offline state and does not imply live control.
If cloud services are unavailable, the local box continues hardware control using local automation.
Deployment and rollback instructions are documented in this plan or linked repo docs.
Relevant backend, frontend, invariant, and deployment checks pass.

validation.command: pnpm --dir web-ui typecheck && pnpm --dir web-ui lint && pnpm --dir web-ui test && pnpm --dir web-ui build
validation.expected_result: Frontend checks and production build pass.
Remote login, dashboard read, stale/offline indicator, command lifecycle, and asset view all behave as documented.

top_level_acceptance_criteria: The frontend is deployable on Vercel or a user-approved equivalent hosted frontend.
The hosted API is thin and never directly controls hardware.
This box remains the only hardware execution authority and uses outbound sync for normal cloud communication.
Cloud state supports one site with multiple tents and does not reintroduce singleton grow assumptions.
Commands are durable, idempotent, expirable, target-scoped, and locally validated before execution.
Photos/video use object storage with signed URLs rather than large Vercel Function payloads.
Offline/stale states are visible to the user and safe actions are disabled when live control cannot be guaranteed.
The plan's progress_log, decision_log, surprises_and_discoveries, and outcomes_and_retrospective are updated by implementation agents.

risks.risk: Moving too much backend logic to Vercel makes hardware safety depend on cloud availability.
risks.mitigation: Keep Vercel as a control plane and enforce that all hardware execution happens locally.

risks.risk: Trying to synchronize every raw reading creates cost and performance problems.
risks.mitigation: Define latest-state and aggregated history contracts; upload only useful raw data where needed.

risks.risk: Media assets exceed serverless request/response limits.
risks.mitigation: Use object storage and signed URLs for photos/video.

risks.risk: Hosted controls appear live while the local gateway is offline.
risks.mitigation: Use last_seen_at, stale flags, command expiration, and UI disabled states.

risks.risk: Provider decisions are made implicitly by whichever agent starts coding first.
risks.mitigation: Require hwcp-01 platform decisions before implementation milestones.

decision_log.decision: Split this work after the multi-tent controller refactor.
decision_log.rationale: Stable local site/tent/zone/device identity reduces cloud/API churn and avoids exposing singleton hardware APIs.
decision_log.consequences: This epic should not begin implementation until docs/epics/multi-tent-controller/plan.json is done or explicitly waived by the user.

decision_log.decision: Use outbound local sync as the normal connectivity model.
decision_log.rationale: The local box controls hardware and should not require inbound router exposure for public website access.
decision_log.consequences: Cloudflare Tunnel may be used as a temporary admin bridge, but not as the core architecture unless a later decision changes this.
